Just a gentle reminder, this is what “AI-native security” looks like in practice.

Anthropic is testing Claude Mythos which is a new early-access model described as a step above Opus and its most capable system yet. 

Anthropic is rolling it out cautiously taking a cautious because the model may carry meaningful cybersecurity risk and is expensive to run.

$META introduced TRIBE v2 which is a brain-response model trained on 500+ hours of fMRI data from 700+ people to predict neural responses to images, video & sound.

Meta is building something that looks increasingly like a proprietary mind map of human attention & perception.

As a follow up, I'm not going to mince words ...

If the real reason for the delay is that the military assets needed were not in place, then don't make up shit about 10 tankers going through the Strait (no evidence) or the Iranians asked for the delay (the Pakistanis, who are the intermediaries, said no such request was made).

Say you'll keep talking, you're losing patience, and say every day the Iranians don't deal means the Marines are getting closer to the Middle East.

The market would understand that, and the crude oil response (fall and stay down) might have been more favorable.

This just looks like a TACO mess.

One day tick chart of crude oil shows this delay did nothing lasting to depress higher crude prices.

The reason is the Iran War was won about two weeks ago. This is now a war to open the Strait. And you just told the market the START of opening the Strait has been pushed off another 10 days. See the repost below.

Twitter likes to talk about TACO, and I do too.

But as @agnostoxxx so eloquently put it, this is now about NACHO.

Not
Actually 
Changing 
Hormuz
Opening

Arm $ARM says that the 200kW liquid cooled racks contain 45,696 Neoverse cores and more than 1PB of low-latency memory, delivering up to 2X performance per rack versus x86 and enabling  “up to $10B in CAPEX savings per GW of AI data center capacity.”

$META $NVDA $AMD $AVGO

Meta will be the chip’s first customer as a lead partner and co-developer, with the AGI CPU working alongside its MTIA accelerators, while OpenAI, Cerebras, Cloudflare and others are also launch partners. Early systems are available now with broader availability in 2H.

$META $ARM $NVDA $NET $AMD

Arm $ARM marked its foray into developing its own chips with the release of its ‘AGI CPU’ chip for agentic AI infrastructure, which will feature up to 136 of its Neoverse cores and offer up to double the performance per rack versus x86 chips. 

$NVDA $AMD $INTC

USDJPY near ~160.

People keep talking about “carry” like it’s free yield.

It’s not.
It’s a short volatility trade with a FX wrapper.

When the unwind starts, it doesn’t ring a bell. It gaps.

The dollar ($DXY) back near ~100.

Every “soft landing” narrative assumes global liquidity is loose.

A firm dollar is a tax on everyone borrowing in it.

Watch who breaks first: EM, credit, or equities.
Unpopular opinion: it's not just a correction, it's a bear market.

It is at your lowest moments, that the devil speaks loudest.

You must not listen.

You must not relent.

Do not relent.

$QQQ around ~574. Roughly ~6% lower than a month ago.

Tech leadership is what everyone *wants*.

But when the leaders start limping, the crowd pretends it’s “rotation.”

No. It’s fragility.

$SPY at ~652. Zoom out: the price is down ~5% from a month ago.
